title: 10 best herbal decoctions for premenstrual syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for premenstrual syndrome are a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms associated with PMS. By steeping herbs in hot water, these decoctions release bioactive compounds that help to regulate hormone levels, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with PMS include chasteberry tea, which helps to balance estrogen levels and reduce mood swings; ginger tea, which eases cramps and bloating; and passionflower tea, which promotes calmness and reduces anxiety. By incorporating these decoctions into their daily routine, women can experience improved mental clarity, reduced physical discomfort, and a overall better quality of life during the premenstrual phase.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it effectively alleviates symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The flavonoids present in hawthorn have been shown to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramps and spasms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce water retention, easing bloating and discomfort. Furthermore, hawthorn's adaptogenic properties can help to calm the nervous system, promoting a sense of balance and relaxation, which is particularly beneficial during this time of hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate PMS symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs): The OPCs in hawthorn have been shown to improve blood vessel function, reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which may help mitigate symptoms like cramps, headaches, and mood changes associated with PMS.

Volatile oils: Hawthorn's volatile oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety, irritability, and insomnia often experienced during the premenstrual phase.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Crataegus monogyna decoctions may help reduce PMS symptoms by acting as estrogen modulators and anti-inflammatory agents, thus alleviating symptoms such as bloating and mood swings.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, including ursolic acid, found in Crataegus monogyna may help alleviate PMS symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could reduce symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood disturbances.

Phenolic acids (e.g., protocatechuic acid): Phenolic acids in Crataegus monogyna decoctions may help alleviate PMS symptoms by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could reduce symptoms such as mood swings and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and tannins, which may help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.

Fruits: They are used because they contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with premenstrual syndrome.
Buds: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce symptoms of anxiety, depression, and other premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of the shrub's branches and leaves in late summer or early autumn.
Dry the harvested material in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 20-30 grams of dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered material with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce breast tenderness and swelling, while its sedative effects calm anxiety and irritability. Additionally, maypop's ability to regulate serotonin levels helps to stabilize mood swings and promotes a sense of relaxation and well-being. As a result, maypop decoctions can provide natural relief from the discomforts of PMS, allowing women to feel more balanced and empowered throughout their menstrual cycles.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop decoctions can help alleviate cramps and spasms associated with PMS by relaxing uterine muscles and reducing menstrual pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce swelling and inflammation in the pelvic region, alleviating symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood changes.
Hormone-regulating properties: Maypop contains flavonoids and alkaloids that may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like mood swings, anxiety, and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with premenstrual syndrome by exhibiting anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties, which can alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and insomnia.
Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-anxiety and sedative effects, which can help reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, including anxiety, irritability, and restlessness.
Harmane: This indole alkaloid has a sedative effect and can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia associated with premenstrual syndrome by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which provide anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Utilized for their rich source of passifloric acid, which exhibits sedative and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate PMS symptoms.
Fruits: Employed for their high concentration of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium, which contribute to stress relief and mood stabilization.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 ounces of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried flowers with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds from the passiflora incarnata.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety, stress, and irritability that often accompany PMS symptoms. The sedative properties of valerian root also help to promote relaxation, which can ease menstrual cramps and mood swings. Additionally, valerian's antispasmodic effects may help alleviate bloating, breast tenderness, and other physical discomforts associated with premenstrual syndrome, providing a sense of relief and well-being for women who experience these symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can alleviate PMS symptoms like breast tenderness and bloating.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's sedative and muscle-relaxing effects help to calm cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with PMS, promoting a sense of relaxation and reduced pain.
GABA-ergic activity: Valerian's ability to modulate GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain helps regulate anxiety, stress, and mood swings, common symptoms of PMS.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: This short-chain fatty acid has sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and restlessness commonly associated with premenstrual syndrome.
Valerenic acid: As a GABA receptor agonist, valerenic acid can enhance the activity of the neurotransmitter GABA, promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and helping to regulate mood swings.
Valeranone: A non-steroidal estrogenic compound, valeranone may help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome by modulating estrogen levels and reducing the severity of premenstrual mood changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and valeranone, which have a sedative effect that helps reduce anxiety and stress associated with PMS.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used in some herbal decoctions as they also contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to the plant's sedative properties.
Fruits: Some herbal preparations include valerian fruits, which contain a lower concentration of sedative compounds but still contribute to the overall effect of the decoction.
remedy_recipe: Dry 1 teaspoon of valeriana officinalis root in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Chop 2 teaspoons of dried valeriana officinalis root into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine 2 teaspoons of chopped valeriana officinalis root with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the valeriana officinalis root mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the valeriana officinalis decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a glass container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its natural ability to balance hormonal fluctuations. The decoction's antimicrobial properties can reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich lime extract can help regulate serotonin levels, alleviating anxiety and depression often associated with PMS. By promoting relaxation and reducing physical discomfort, herbal lime decoctions can provide relief from a range of premenstrual symptoms, allowing women to feel more comfortable and confident throughout their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lime decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as mood swings, fatigue, and breast tenderness.

Uterine relaxant and anti-spasmodic properties: The decoction's uterine relaxing effects can help ease cramps, bloating, and other physical discomforts associated with PMS, while its anti-spasmodic properties can reduce muscle cramping and spasms.

Hormone-balancing properties: Herbal lime decoctions contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which are thought to contribute to many PMS symptoms, such as mood changes, irritability, and anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as anxiety, mood swings, and physical discomfort.

Triterpenoids (specifically Ursolic acid): This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of premenstrual syndrome symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood changes.
Tiliaflavone (a flavone glycoside): Tiliaflavone has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia often associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, which help alleviate symptoms of PMS.
Buds: They are used for their calming effects, which can help reduce anxiety and emotional symptoms associated with PMS.
Flowers: They are used for their mild sedative and antidepressant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, irritability, and mood swings associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried tilia x europaea flowers and 1 liter of water for the decoction.
Combine the flowers with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Allow the decoction to cool before serving in doses of 20-50ml as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that often accompany PMS symptoms. The herb's flavonoids also help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to mood swings, bloating, and breast tenderness. Additionally, lemon balm decoctions have been shown to ease cramps and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate physical discomfort associated with PMS. Overall, it provides a natural and soothing approach to managing premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help relieve cramping and spasms in the uterus, which can alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with PMS.

Anxiolytic and stress-reducing effects: Lemon balm's calming and relaxing properties can help reduce anxiety, tension, and mood swings often experienced during PMS, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to symptoms like breast tenderness, bloating, and fatigue associated with PMS.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate PMS symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in stress and mood regulation.
Glycosides (e.g., melissoside and isomelissoside): These compounds have a sedative effect, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and irritability commonly experienced during PMS.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has a relaxing effect, which may help reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia associated with PMS by modulating the activity of GABA receptors in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melissa officinalis leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.

Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are used to make teas and infusions that can help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and mood swings, due to their calming and sedative effects.

Roots: Melissa officinalis roots are used to make decoctions that can help reduce menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for a standard dose of decoction.
Rinse the harvested melissa officinalis in cold running water for 2 minutes to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed melissa officinalis with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Simmer the mixture at 95 to 99 degrees celsius for 5 to 10 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the melissa officinalis solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating associated with PMS. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenes work together to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramps and discomfort. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce water retention and alleviate feelings of fatigue, leaving women feeling more balanced and prepared for their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Maidenhair tree decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate physical symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in maidenhair tree decoctions can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to the development of PMS symptoms.

Hormone-regulating property: Maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms related to hormonal imbalance, such as mood swings, anxiety, and irritability.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate premenstrual syndrome symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms like mood swings and headaches.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with premenstrual syndrome, thereby alleviating symptoms like anxiety and depression.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide has been found to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate premenstrual syndrome symptoms like mood swings, anxiety, and depression by reducing inflammation and promoting neural health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which helps alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are utilized for their potential to balance hormonal fluctuations, which can contribute to premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used in decoctions due to their possible influence on hormonal regulation and relief from premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 5-10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and transfer them to a clean glass container.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 15-30 minutes or until the liquid is reduced to 250 milliliters.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before transferring it to a dark-tinted glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains compounds that have a positive effect on hormone regulation, reducing symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The antioxidants present in rosemary also help to alleviate cramps and spasms by relaxing the uterine muscles. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce water retention, leading to a decrease in overall discomfort associated with PMS. Overall, incorporating herbal rosemary decoctions into one's routine may provide natural relief from premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Rosemary's antispasmodic effect helps to relieve cramps, spasms, and stiffness associated with PMS, by relaxing muscle contractions.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort in the breasts, abdomen, and joints often experienced during PMS.

Antioxidant property: Rosemary's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that contribute to hormonal imbalances, mood swings, and other symptoms associated with PMS, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as mood swings and breast tenderness, by blocking the production of prostaglandins.

Carnosic acid: A diterpenoid compound found in rosemary, carnosic acid exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and fatigue.
Bornyl acetate: A monoterpenoid compound, bornyl acetate has been shown to have a sedative effect, which may help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and insomnia.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which can help reduce symptoms of PMS such as anxiety and bloating.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS like cramps and mood swings.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, a compound with antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate PMS symptoms such as cramps and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 30 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and chop them finely to release their oils.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have a positive impact on mood and emotional well-being. As the symptoms of PMS can include irritability, anxiety, and depression, st john's wort may help to alleviate these feelings by increasing levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine in the brain. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may also help to reduce inflammation and other physical symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant/Anxiolytic compounds: St John's Wort contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression associated with PMS, such as mood swings and irritability.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate physical symptoms of PMS, such as breast tenderness, bloating, and cramps, by reducing inflammation in the body.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort's antioxidant properties can help mitigate oxidative stress and damage caused by hormonal fluctuations during the premenstrual phase, which may contribute to mood disturbances and other PMS symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, helping to regulate mood and alleviate symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and depression.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Kaempferol: Demonstrates estrogenic activity, which can help balance hormonal fluctuations during the menstrual cycle, reducing symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate PMS symptoms.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a balancing effect on hormonal changes associated with PMS.
Roots: They are a source of hypericin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce PMS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-50 grams of fresh or dried flowers and leaves of hypericum perforatum for a standard decoction.
Chop the gathered herbs into smaller pieces to release their active compounds for better extraction.
Combine the chopped herbs with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow for efficient extraction of active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms such as bloating and breast tenderness. The decoction's ability to stimulate digestion and reduce water retention also helps to alleviate cramps and mood swings associated with PMS. Additionally, chicory's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to hormonal fluctuations, reducing stress and anxiety often experienced during this time. remedy_properties: Saponins: Chicory saponins have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate anxiety and irritability often experienced during PMS.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in chicory decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce breast tenderness and swelling commonly associated with PMS.
Inulin: Inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in chicory, can help regulate gut bacteria and alleviate symptoms of bloating, cramps, and digestive discomfort often experienced during PMS.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin helps alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome by regulating blood sugar levels and promoting digestion, which can help reduce bloating and other symptoms associated with PMS.

Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, apigenin can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with PMS by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain and modulating the body's response to stress.

Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin can help reduce symptoms of PMS by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to symptoms such as mood swings and breast tenderness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
Roots: Contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
Flowers: Used as a diuretic, which can help alleviate bloating and water retention associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh cichorium intybus leaves and flowers for decoction.
Dry the harvested cichorium intybus in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried cichorium intybus with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Boil the cichorium intybus mixture for 5-7 minutes then reduce heat to a simmer.
Strain the cichorium intybus decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve after 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of PMS, such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties may help to reduce water retention and relieve cramping. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Wormwood's antispasmodic properties can help alleviate cramping and spasms associated with PMS, providing relief from menstrual pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which is often exacerbated by hormonal fluctuations during PMS, leading to symptoms such as bloating and breast tenderness.

remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone has anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and mood swings associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS).

Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce symptoms of bloating, breast tenderness, and other physical discomforts experienced during PMS.

Roots: The roots of Artemisia absinthium are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium contain essential oils and flavonoids that may help regulate hormones and alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers from a trusted herbal source.
Crush the herb using a mortar and pestle to release its active constituents into a fine powder.
Combine the crushed herb with 1 quart of boiling water to create a decoction.
Allow the decoction to steep for 10 to 15 minutes to release the herb's active properties into the water.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids from the liquid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla and measure out one teaspoon per cup.
Combine harvested herbs with boiling water in a saucepan and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Take two to three cups of the cooled decoction as needed during premenstrual symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried ziziphus jujuba fruit 20 grams and filtered water 2 liters.
Combine the ingredients in a large pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes allowing the flavors to infuse slowly.
Strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of bloating and cramping associated with PMS.
Barks: Contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate cramps, breast tenderness, and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried avena sativa oat straw per cup of boiling water.
Measure two cups of cold filtered water and pour it over the harvested oats in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and then store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it alleviates symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. Digestive-soothing property: Peppermint decoctions can calm digestive issues like bloating, gas, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), common complaints among women experiencing PMS symptoms.
Antispasmodic property: The menthol in peppermint helps relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that can occur during the premenstrual phase.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to ease cramps, spasms, and pain associated with PMS.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation, alleviate bloating, and soothe digestive discomfort commonly experienced during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of water needed.
Boil the measured water in a pot for five minutes to create a rolling boil.
Add the dried peppermint leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes or until the flavor has been infused fully.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain flavonoids, terpenes, and volatile oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms of PMS. Additionally, hops have been shown to inhibit the activity of histamine, a neurotransmitter that can contribute to mood swings and irritability. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal hops decoctions may help alleviate physical and emotional symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Hops contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, helping to alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, irritability, and insomnia associated with PMS.
Antispasmodic property: The alpha-bitter acids present in hops help relax smooth muscle tissue, which can ease cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness often experienced during PMS.

Estrogen-regulating property: Hops have been found to possess estrogen-like properties, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms such as mood swings, water retention, and breast pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing saponins and flavonoids, the roots of the hops plant are used to alleviate anxiety, restlessness, and insomnia associated with PMS.
Buds: Rich in essential oils, hops buds have a calming effect on the nervous system and help reduce symptoms of irritability and mood swings during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus herb with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes then strain and discard the solids.
Allow the liquid to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for at least 30 minutes.
Consume 1 cup of the decoction three times daily starting 10 days before menstruation and continuing throughout your period.
Store any leftover decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and discard after use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has also been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances, reducing symptoms like anxiety, irritability, and breast tenderness. Additionally, the decoction's sweet, soothing flavor can calm digestive issues and promote relaxation, helping to reduce overall discomfort during this time of month.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic activity: Licorice root has adaptogenic properties, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and hormonal fluctuations during PMS, reducing symptoms such as anxiety and mood swings.

Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects: The decoction's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness associated with PMS by relaxing muscles and reducing inflammation in the body.

Hormone regulation: Licorice root has been shown to have hormone-balancing properties, which can help regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of PMS such as mood swings, fatigue, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate symptoms of PMS such as mood swings and bloating.
Rhyzomes: Contain isoflavones that have been shown to reduce the severity of premenstrual syndrome symptoms by regulating hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root fragments.
Boil 250 milliliters of distilled water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the weighed root fragments to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Simmer the mixture for 15 minutes to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica decoctions helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its ability to balance hormonal fluctuations and reduce symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness. The natural compounds in angelica root, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, have been shown to modulate estrogen levels, alleviating the discomfort associated with PMS. Additionally, angelica's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe digestive issues and reduce water retention, further alleviating symptoms. As a result, angelica decoctions can provide natural relief from the physical and emotional symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic properties: Angelica's decoction helps to relax uterine muscles and alleviate cramps, reducing symptoms like lower abdominal pain, backaches, and breast tenderness.
Hormone-regulating effects: Angelica contains compounds that can help regulate estrogen levels, which can help alleviate PMS symptoms such as mood swings, irritability, and anxiety.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica's decoction has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with PMS symptoms like breast tenderness and joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of coumarins, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as bloating and cramps.
Barks: The barks of the angelica plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat menstrual cramps, making it an effective addition to decoctions for PMS relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica archangelica root and leaves from a reputable source in an amount of 20-30 grams per serving.
Combine the dried herb with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes to release the active compounds and create a flavorful brew.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool and then refrigerate it at 39 degrees fahrenheit or below for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal decoctions are menopausal symptoms, endometriosis, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Herbal remedies such as red clover, black cohosh, and dong quai help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause. Decoctions of turmeric, ginger, and chamomile may reduce inflammation and cramping in endometriosis sufferers, while herbs like chasteberry and vitex may regulate hormones and improve symptoms in PCOS patients. These natural remedies offer a holistic approach to managing women's health issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with premenstrual syndrome is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Chamomile. Peppermint helps to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate cramps and bloating. Chamomile calms the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Another beneficial addition is the decoction of Red Clover, which balances hormonal fluctuations and eases symptoms of PMS. These herbal combinations work synergistically to alleviate PMS symptoms, promoting a smoother and more comfortable menstrual cycle.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by BJOG : an international journal of obstetrics and gynaecology, herbal decoctions, specifically Hypericum perforatum, have shown potential in alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome in women. The study, conducted by the Department of Complementary Medicine at the University of Exeter, involved 19 women with premenstrual syndrome who took hypericum tablets for two menstrual cycles. The results showed significant reductions in all outcome measures, with a 51% improvement in overall premenstrual syndrome scores between baseline and the end of the trial, and over two-thirds of the sample demonstrating at least a 50% decrease in symptom severity.
